formulas.name: Content
formulas.formula:
Here's the complete formula sheet for CBSE Class 11 Chemistry - The p-Block Elements (Groups 13 & 14):

```json
[
  {
    "name": "Boron Trifluoride (BF3) structure",
    "formula": "BF3: B at center; three F-atoms at corners of equilateral triangle",
    "variables": "B = Boron, F = Fluorine",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "BF3 is a trigonal planar molecule."
  },
  {
    "name": "Aluminum Chloride (AlCl3) structure",
    "formula": "AlCl3: Al at center; three Cl-atoms around Al in triangular pyramidal structure",
    "variables": "Al = Aluminum, Cl = Chlorine",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "AlCl3 is a trigonal pyramidal molecule."
  },
  {
    "name": "Diborane (B2H6) structure",
    "formula": "B2H6: Two B-atoms connected by two bridge hydrogen atoms, each B connected to two terminal hydrogen atoms",
    "variables": "B = Boron, H = Hydrogen",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Diborane is a highly unstable compound."
  },
  {
    "name": "Carbonates and Bicarbonates of Group 13",
    "formula": "M2CO3 + H2O → 2MOH + H2CO3",
    "variables": "M = Group 13 element (e.g., Al, Ga)",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Carbonates are less stable than bicarbonates in Group 13."
  },
  {
    "name": "Group 14 - Carbon Family",
    "formula": "The carbon family (Group 14) elements are: C, Si, Ge, Sn, and Pb.",
    "variables": "C = Carbon, Si = Silicon, Ge = Germanium, Sn = Tin, Pb = Lead",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "The carbon family elements exhibit a gradual increase in atomic size and a decrease in electronegativity."
  },
  {
    "name": "Group 13 - Boron Family",
    "formula": "The boron family (Group 13) elements are: B, Al, Ga, In, and Tl.",
    "variables": "B = Boron, Al = Aluminum, Ga = Gallium, In = Indium, Tl = Thallium",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "The boron family elements exhibit a gradual increase in atomic size and a decrease in electronegativity."
  },
  {
    "name": "Group 14 - Carbon Family Trends",
    "formula": "Atomic radius increases, electronegativity decreases, and ionization enthalpy decreases as you move down Group 14.",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "These trends are due to the gradual filling of the valence shell of Group 14 elements."
  },
  {
    "name": "Group 13 - Boron Family Trends",
    "formula": "Atomic radius increases, electronegativity decreases, and ionization enthalpy decreases as you move down Group 13.",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "These trends are due to the gradual filling of the valence shell of Group 13 elements."
  },
  {
    "name": "Oxides of Group 13",
    "formula": "M2O3 + H2O → 2MOH + H2O2",
    "variables": "M = Group 13 element (e.g., Al, Ga)",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Oxides of Group 13 elements are amphoteric."
  },
  {
    "name": "Oxides of Group 14",
    "formula": "MO2 + H2O → M(OH)2",
    "variables": "M = Group 14 element (e.g., C, Si)",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Oxides of Group 14 elements are amphoteric."
  },
  {
    "name": "Carbonates and Bicarbonates of Group 14",
    "formula": "M2CO3 + H2O → 2MOH + H2CO3",
    "variables": "M = Group 14 element (e.g., C, Si)",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Carbonates are less stable than bicarbonates in Group 14."
  }
]
```

Note: This JSON array includes formulas and statements related to the p-Block Elements (Groups 13 & 14) chapter in the CBSE Class 11 Chemistry syllabus.
